message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: CHARTER status says infrastructure seams are "stubs being wired" — contradicts README/ADR-0010 and the actual (implemented, tested) code
DIMENSION: docs vs code drift / over-claims
SEVERITY: MEDIUM
FILE: docs/CHARTER.md:3 3
DESCRIPTION: CHARTER.md L3 status line reads: "Status: Active development — engine skeleton and methodology core implemented (see ARCHITECTURE.md / ADR-0002); infrastructure seams are stubs being wired." This is stale and contradicts both the README and the code. README L10-15 states the seams are "in place and hardened ... all five infrastructure seams (tiered reasoning, self-hosted observability, persistence, orchestration, baseline toolkit) ... a single composed runner — run_study." The code confirms the README, not the CHARTER: reasoning/seam.py, observability/tracing.py + tracking.py, persistence/{checkpoint,vectorstore,versioning}.py, orchestration/{graph,gates,gatenode,phases}.py, and baseline/* are all fully implemented (not stubs), with run_study composing them and 407 tests passing (5 skipped). ADR-0010 (Accepted 2026-06-16) documents the seams as "ported and individually live-verified" and composed. The CHARTER status is the only doc still describing the seams as unfinished stubs.
SUGGESTED FIX: Update CHARTER.md L3 status to match reality and the README, e.g. "Status: Engine implemented and composed — methodology core, all five infrastructure seams, append-only provenance, and the composed run_study runner are in place and hardened (ADR-0010)."
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# hc-assay — Charter
2	
3	**Status:** Active development — engine skeleton and methodology core implemented (see
4	ARCHITECTURE.md / ADR-0002); infrastructure seams are stubs being wired.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 1. Purpose
9	
10	`hc-assay` is a reusable blueprint — an engine plus a methodology — for conducting
11	**rigorous, reproducible empirical machine-learning and NLP analysis of security & privacy
12	data.** It is designed to be cloned onto any dataset in that domain and produce findings
13	that a hostile reviewer can reproduce, not opinions that must be taken on trust.
14	
15	The blueprint is dataset-agnostic. It makes no assumption about any particular corpus,
16	authority, taxonomy, or set of claims. Everything dataset-specific lives in an **adapter**
17	and in the **instance repository** for a given study — never in the engine.
18	
19	## 2. Principles
20

1	# hc-assay
2	
3	**A reusable blueprint for rigorous, reproducible empirical ML/NLP on security & privacy data.**
4	
5	`hc-assay` is not a single study. It is an **engine + a methodology** that you clone onto a
6	dataset to build an independent, empirical understanding of it — and, where the dataset
7	carries external expert-asserted claims, to adjudicate those claims against that
8	independent understanding.
9	
10	Status: **engine implemented and composed.** The dataset-agnostic engine (`src/assay_engine`)
11	is in place and hardened — the contracts, the methodology core (hypotheses, three verdicts, the
12	two firewalls, content-bound pre-registration, the measurement↔interpretation fence), all five
13	infrastructure seams (tiered reasoning, self-hosted observability, persistence, orchestration,
14	baseline toolkit), an append-only hash-chained provenance trail, and a single composed runner —
15	`run_study` — that ties them into one governed end-to-end flow (ADR-0010). The engine core is
16	dependency-free; heavy backends are lazy-imported behind optional extras (`reasoning`,
17	`observability`, `persistence`, `orchestration`, `baseline`, or `all`) so it installs, imports,
18	and unit-tests offline (ADR-0006). You build a study by implementing the adapter Protocols for
19	your dataset and calling `run_study`.
20	
21	## Quickstart
22	
23	```bash
24	pip install assay-engine            # dependency-free core
25	pip install "assay-engine[all]"     # + every optional backend
26	```
27	
28	```python
29	from assay_engine import StudyPlan, run_study, auto_approve
30

content.content:
1	# ADR-0010 — The engine composes and governs the end-to-end study run
2	
3	**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-16)
4	
5	## Context
6	
7	By ADR-0008/0009 the engine owned each *guarantee* in isolation — the firewalls, pre-registration
8	— and the five infra seams (reasoning, orchestration, observability, persistence, baseline) were
9	ported and individually live-verified. But nothing **composed** them into one runnable flow. The
10	phase machine (`orchestration/phases.py`) *named* the ordered stages; `gates.py` even noted that
11	"the append-only provenance store … [is] wired by the orchestration layer when it lands." Until
12	then, "the workflow" — ingest → blind baseline → discover/confirm → adjudicate/score → report,
13	with the governance handoffs between them — existed only as discipline each cloning study would
14	have to re-implement. That is precisely the per-clone footgun ADR-0005/0008 exist to remove, and
15	it meant there was no end-to-end artifact to make public with confidence.
16	
17	Two concrete gaps:
18	- No executor walked the phases, enforced the order, and wired the methodology runners + seams.
19	- The GOVERNANCE §3 append-only provenance trail did not exist at all.
20	
21	## Decision
22	
23	1. **Append-only provenance trail** (`assay_engine/provenance.py`). A `ProvenanceTrail` records
24	   every action — run start, ingest, baseline, each discovered *and* claim-derived locked
25	   hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict, the scorecard, the report — *before the next

content.text:
Confirmed: 407 passed, 5 skipped — exactly as the assessor and README claim.

All facts verified:
- CHARTER.md L3-4 says "infrastructure seams are stubs being wired."
- README L10-15 says seams are "in place and hardened" and composed via `run_study`.
- All five seam directories contain full implementations (e.g., seam.py is 652 LOC), not stubs.
- The only NotImplementedError instances are deliberate fail-loud "Unconfigured" sentinels (documented pattern), not unfinished work.
- pipeline.py (`run_study`) imports and composes all five seams + the provenance trail.
- ADR-0010 (Accepted 2026-06-16) documents the seams as "ported and individually live-verified" and now composed.
- Test suite: 407 passed, 5 skipped.

The CHARTER status line is genuinely stale and contradicts the README, ADR-0010, and the implemented/tested code. However, I'll calibrate severity carefully: this is a documentation-only drift in a single status line. It does not affect engine behavior, correctness, security, or installability. For a project being made public for hostile-reviewer scrutiny, the charter being the headline governance doc that contradicts the README is a real credibility defect — but it is squarely a docs/over-claims-inversion issue, not a runtime production-readiness defect. MEDIUM as reported is slightly high; LOW is the accurate calibration for a single stale status line that is internally contradicted by the project's own README and ADR.

input.reasoning: VERIFIED TRUE, but recalibrated MEDIUM -> LOW. The claim is factually accurate on every point. CHARTER.md L3-4 still reads "infrastructure seams are stubs being wired," which is stale and directly contradicted by (a) README.md L10-15 ("engine implemented and composed ... all five infrastructure seams ... in place and hardened ... a single composed runner — run_study"), (b) ADR-0010 (Accepted 2026-06-16, at docs/decisions/ADR-0010-engine-composed-study-runner.md), which describes the five seams as "ported and individually live-verified" and now composed, and (c) the actual code. I confirmed all five seam packages contain full implementations (reasoning/seam.py is 652 LOC, persistence/checkpoint.py 240 LOC, orchestration/graph.py 143 LOC, plus observability/tracing.py+tracking.py and baseline/*). The only NotImplementedError occurrences (reasoning/seam.py:649, observability/tracing.py:212) are intentional fail-loud "Unconfigured" sentinels (UnconfiguredReasoningSeam, UnconfiguredTracer), not unfinished stubs. pipeline.py's run_study imports and composes all five seams plus the append-only ProvenanceTrail. Test suite confirms maturity: 407 passed, 5 skipped. So the CHARTER status is the sole doc still describing the seams as unfinished — a genuine docs-vs-code over-claim inversion. Severity reduced to LOW because this is a single stale status line in a documentation file: it has zero impact on engine behavior, correctness, security, install/import, or test results, and is already contradicted by the project's own primary README and an Accepted ADR (so a reviewer reaches the correct picture immediately). It is a credibility/hygiene defect worth fixing before going public, not a runtime production-readiness defect; MEDIUM overstates operational risk.
input.evidence: docs/CHARTER.md L3-4: "**Status:** Active development — engine skeleton and methodology core implemented (see ARCHITECTURE.md / ADR-0002); infrastructure seams are stubs being wired." CONTRADICTED BY README.md L10-15: "Status: **engine implemented and composed.** ... all five infrastructure seams (tiered reasoning, self-hosted observability, persistence, orchestration, baseline toolkit) ... a single composed runner — run_study ... (ADR-0010)." Code evidence: `wc -l` -> reasoning/seam.py 652, persistence/checkpoint.py 240, orchestration/graph.py 143; all of observability/{tracing,tracking}.py, orchestration/{gates,gatenode,graph,phases}.py, persistence/{checkpoint,vectorstore,versioning}.py, baseline/{toolkit,primitives,determinism}.py present and implemented. The only two NotImplementedError are deliberate sentinels: reasoning/seam.py:645 `class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam` ("supply a TieredReasoningSeam ... to perform reasoning") and tracing.py:205 `class UnconfiguredTracer` ("no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing()"). pipeline.py:194 `def run_study(...)` imports baseline.toolkit, observability.tracing+tracking, orchestration.gates+phases, persistence.versioning, provenance.ProvenanceTrail and composes them. ADR-0010 (docs/decisions/ADR-0010-engine-composed-study-runner.md) Status: Accepted (2026-06-16), Context: seams "ported and individually live-verified." pytest: "407 passed, 5 skipped, 53 warnings in 6.37s."

content.text:
Verified. The finding is TRUE but I recalibrated severity from MEDIUM to LOW.

Summary: CHARTER.md L3-4 genuinely is stale ("infrastructure seams are stubs being wired") and contradicts the README, ADR-0010, and the implemented/tested code. I confirmed all five seam packages are fully implemented (not stubs — the only NotImplementedError instances are deliberate fail-loud Unconfigured* sentinels), that `run_study` in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` composes them all plus the provenance trail, that ADR-0010 (Accepted 2026-06-16) exists at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/docs/decisions/ADR-0010-engine-composed-study-runner.md`, and that the test suite passes 407/5-skipped.

I lowered severity to LOW because it is a single stale documentation status line with zero impact on behavior/correctness/security/install, and it is already contradicted by the project's own README and an Accepted ADR — a credibility/hygiene fix, not a runtime production-readiness defect. The assessor's suggested fix is sound.
